papers.title: Axicabtagene Ciloleucel as Second-Line Therapy for Large B-Cell Lymphoma (ZUMA-7)
grounds: 2L CAR-T beats salvage-chemo/ASCT in primary-refractory/early-relapse LBCL: axi-cel ZUMA-7 primary EFS

papers.title: Survival with Axicabtagene Ciloleucel in Large B-Cell Lymphoma (ZUMA-7 OS update)
grounds: 2L CAR-T beats salvage-chemo/ASCT: axi-cel ZUMA-7 overall survival update (positive OS)

papers.title: Lisocabtagene maraleucel versus standard of care with salvage chemotherapy followed by ASCT as second-line treatment in relapsed/refractory large B-cell lymphoma (TRANSFORM): interim analysis, phase 3
grounds: 2L CAR-T beats salvage-chemo/ASCT: liso-cel TRANSFORM

papers.title: Lisocabtagene maraleucel as second-line therapy in adults with relapsed or refractory large B-cell lymphoma not intended for HSCT (PILOT): open-label phase 2
grounds: liso-cel PILOT: 2L, transplant-ineligible, single-arm

papers.title: Second-Line Tisagenlecleucel or Standard Care in Aggressive B-Cell Lymphoma (BELINDA)
grounds: tisa-cel BELINDA: 2L NEGATIVE EFS, encodes 'not 2L' for tisa-cel

papers.title: Lisocabtagene maraleucel for patients with relapsed or refractory large B-cell lymphomas (TRANSCEND NHL 001): a multicentre seamless design study
grounds: TRANSCEND NHL 001: liso-cel pivotal 3L+; permitted secondary CNS lymphoma

papers.title: Outcomes in refractory diffuse large B-cell lymphoma: results from the international SCHOLAR-1 study
grounds: SCHOLAR-1: refractory DLBCL, median OS ~6 mo; pre-CAR-T baseline prognosis

papers.title: Autologous bone marrow transplantation as compared with salvage chemotherapy in relapses of chemotherapy-sensitive non-Hodgkin's lymphoma (PARMA)
grounds: ASCT requires chemosensitivity (canonical randomized evidence: benefit only in chemotherapy-sensitive relapse)

papers.title: Therapeutic options for large B-cell lymphoma relapsing after CD19-directed CAR T-cell therapy
papers.authors_year: El Warrak S, Kharfan-Dabaja MA, Mohty R, et al., 2023
grounds: allo-HCT reserved post-CAR-T (review anchor); attainment of CR prior to allografting is prerequisite for durable remission

papers.title: Tafasitamab for patients with relapsed or refractory diffuse large B-cell lymphoma: final 5-year efficacy and safety findings in the phase II L-MIND study
grounds: Tafasitamab-lenalidomide L-MIND 5-year final analysis (grounding for the ASCT-ineligible R/R DLBCL claim; note L-MIND excluded primary-refractory disease)

papers.title: Tafasitamab plus lenalidomide in relapsed or refractory diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (L-MIND): a multicentre, prospective, single-arm, phase 2 study
grounds: Original L-MIND primary publication behind the tafasitamab-lenalidomide approval

papers.title: Mosunetuzumab Plus Polatuzumab Vedotin in Transplant-Ineligible Refractory/Relapsed Large B-Cell Lymphoma: Primary Results of the Phase III SUNMO Trial
grounds: Phase 3 SUNMO of mosunetuzumab + polatuzumab vedotin vs R-GemOx in ASCT-ineligible R/R LBCL (mPFS 11.5 vs 3.8 mo, HR 0.41)

papers.title: Mosunetuzumab with polatuzumab vedotin in relapsed or refractory aggressive large B cell lymphoma: a phase 1b/2 trial
grounds: Earlier phase 1b/2 of mosunetuzumab + polatuzumab vedotin in R/R aggressive LBCL (superseded as evidence by phase 3 SUNMO)

papers.title: Epcoritamab plus GemOx in transplant-ineligible relapsed/refractory DLBCL: results from the EPCORE NHL-2 trial
grounds: Epcoritamab + GemOx EPCORE NHL-2, off-label 2L combo (ORR 85%, CR 61%)

papers.title: AbbVie/Genmab Announce Topline Results for Epcoritamab from Phase 3 EPCORE DLBCL-1 Trial in Relapsed/Refractory DLBCL
grounds: Epcoritamab monotherapy phase 3 EPCORE DLBCL-1 topline confirmatory trial (PFS HR 0.74, 95% CI 0.60-0.92); press-release only, no peer-reviewed publication as of Jul 2026

papers.title: Epcoritamab, a Novel, Subcutaneous CD3xCD20 Bispecific T-Cell-Engaging Antibody, in Relapsed or Refractory Large B-Cell Lymphoma: Dose Expansion in a Phase I/II Trial
grounds: Pivotal EPCORE NHL-1 dose-expansion behind epcoritamab monotherapy 3L+ approval (ORR 63.1%, CR 38.9%)

papers.title: Glofitamab plus gemcitabine and oxaliplatin (GemOx) versus rituximab-GemOx for relapsed or refractory diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (STARGLO): a global phase 3, randomised, open-label trial
grounds: Phase 3 STARGLO of glofitamab + GemOx vs R-GemOx (OS HR 0.59; the trial that received the FDA CRL 2025-07-18)

papers.title: Glofitamab for Relapsed or Refractory Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma
grounds: Glofitamab monotherapy pivotal trial behind the 3L+ approval (fixed-duration; CR 39%)

papers.title: Polatuzumab Vedotin in Relapsed or Refractory Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma (GO29365)
grounds: Pola-BR GO29365 randomized phase 2 (pola-BR vs BR)
Polatuzumab vedotin (MMAE, anti-microtubule) causes cumulative peripheral neuropathy (PN 43.6% grade 1-2, cumulative onset) that can stack with/exacerbate prior vincristine neurotoxicity

FDA POLIVY Prescribing Information (regulatory label, no PMID): PN is a cumulative effect and POLIVY may exacerbate pre-existing peripheral neuropathy — supports label/safety framing (accessdata.fda.gov/drugsatfda_docs/label/2019/761121s000lbl.pdf)

papers.title: Loncastuximab tesirine in relapsed or refractory diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (LOTIS-2): a multicentre, open-label, single-arm, phase 2 trial
grounds: Loncastuximab tesirine LOTIS-2 pivotal phase 2 behind the 3L+ approval (ORR 48.3%, CR 24.1%)

papers.title: Simplified Geriatric Assessment in Older Patients With Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma: The Prospective Elderly Project of the Fondazione Italiana Linfomi
papers.authors_year: Merli F, Luminari S, Tucci A, Spina M, et al., 2021
grounds: FIL simplified Geriatric Assessment (sGA) classifying fit/unfit/frail in elderly DLBCL
Prospective elderly-DLBCL sGA validation (Merli/Spina); feeds the Elderly Prognostic Index (EPI)

papers.title: Practical Assessment and Management of Vulnerabilities in Older Patients Receiving Chemotherapy: ASCO Guideline for Geriatric Oncology
papers.authors_year: Mohile SG, Dale W, Somerfield MR, Hurria A, et al., 2018
grounds: ASCO Geriatric Oncology guideline (2018 Mohile) — primary full guideline grounding GA-guided management of older cancer patients

papers.title: Practical Assessment and Management of Vulnerabilities in Older Patients Receiving Systemic Cancer Therapy: ASCO Guideline Update
papers.authors_year: Dale W, Klepin HD, Williams GR, Mohile SG, et al., 2023
grounds: ASCO Geriatric Oncology guideline 2023 update
Recommends GA-guided management (GAM) for all 65+ receiving systemic therapy; endorses the Practical Geriatric Assessment

papers.title: Use of comprehensive geriatric assessment in older cancer patients: recommendations from the task force on CGA of the International Society of Geriatric Oncology (SIOG)
papers.authors_year: Extermann M, Aapro M, Bernabei R, Topinkova E, et al., 2005
grounds: SIOG recommendations on (comprehensive) geriatric assessment in older cancer patients — canonical SIOG task-force document

papers.title: Attenuated immunochemotherapy regimen (R-miniCHOP) in elderly patients older than 80 years with diffuse large B-cell lymphoma: a multicentre, single-arm, phase 2 trial
papers.authors_year: Peyrade F, Jardin F, Thieblemont C, Tilly H, et al., 2011
grounds: R-miniCHOP for DLBCL >=80 (Peyrade, LYSA/GELA) — grounds 'age-attenuated 1L is valid, not under-dosing'
Concludes R-miniCHOP should be the standard treatment for DLBCL patients >80y

papers.title: Rating chronic medical illness burden in geropsychiatric practice and research: application of the Cumulative Illness Rating Scale
papers.authors_year: Miller MD, Paradis CF, Houck PR, Reynolds CF, et al., 1992
grounds: CIRS-G (Cumulative Illness Rating Scale for Geriatrics) — canonical methods/manual reference for the comorbidity index embedded in FIL GA

papers.title: A new method of classifying prognostic comorbidity in longitudinal studies: development and validation
papers.authors_year: Charlson ME, Pompei P, Ales KL, MacKenzie CR, 1987
grounds: Charlson Comorbidity Index — canonical original development/validation reference for the comorbidity index used in these decisions

papers.title: Toxicity and efficacy of chimeric antigen receptor T-cell therapy in patients with diffuse large B-cell lymphoma above the age of 70 years compared to younger patients — a matched control multicenter cohort study
papers.authors_year: Ram R, Grisariu S, Shargian-Alon L, Avivi I, et al., 2022
grounds: CAR-T in older adults >=70: matched feasibility/efficacy series grounding 'no numeric age cutoff; fitness+logistics composite'
Comparable CRS/ICANS, response, and survival vs younger; age per se should not preclude CAR-T

papers.title: Anti-CD19 CAR-T Cell Therapy in Elderly Patients: Multicentric Real-World Experience from GETH-TC/GELTAMO
papers.authors_year: Bailén R, Iacoboni G, Delgado J, Kwon M, et al., 2024
grounds: CAR-T in older adults >=70: larger real-world series (n=412 infused; 71 >=70) showing comparable efficacy to younger, no numeric age cutoff
Age >=70 independent risk factor for grade >=3 CRS but not for PFS/OS — supports fitness+logistics composite over age threshold

papers.title: Geriatric assessment-identified deficits in older cancer patients with normal performance status
papers.authors_year: Jolly TA, Deal AM, Nyrop KA, Muss HB, et al., 2015
grounds: Evidence that performance status (ECOG/KPS) under-captures frailty vs multi-domain geriatric assessment
Among 796 functionally normal patients (KPS >=80), 69% had >=1 GA-identified deficit and 24% had >=3

papers.title: MATRix-RICE therapy and autologous haematopoietic stem-cell transplantation in diffuse large B-cell lymphoma with secondary CNS involvement (MARIETTA): an international, single-arm, phase 2 trial
grounds: Secondary CNS lymphoma treatment: MARIETTA regimen (MATRix induction -> R-ICE/RICE -> carmustine-thiotepa + autologous HSCT) in DLBCL with concurrent or relapsed CNS involvement

papers.title: Chemoimmunotherapy with methotrexate, cytarabine, thiotepa, and rituximab (MATRix regimen) in patients with primary CNS lymphoma: results of the first randomisation of the IELSG32 phase 2 trial
grounds: MATRix regimen (HD-MTX + cytarabine + thiotepa + rituximab) as the CNS-active chemoimmunotherapy backbone; IELSG32 first randomisation establishing MATRix as standard induction

papers.title: A focus on pharmacotherapy for primary central nervous system lymphoma
grounds: CNS pharmacology: HD-MTX (>=3 g/m2), high-dose cytarabine, thiotepa achieve cytotoxic CSF/parenchymal levels; supports why ara-C-containing salvage (R-DHAP) is more CNS-active than R-ICE. NOTE: PCNSL-framed; does not itself headline 'R-CHOP components/cisplatin fail to reach cytotoxic CNS levels'

papers.title: The 5th edition of the World Health Organization Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours: Lymphoid Neoplasms
grounds: WHO-HAEM5 lymphoid classification framework; entity definitions used to distinguish double-hit high-grade B-cell lymphoma (MYC AND BCL2 rearrangements) from MYC amplification and double-expressor phenotype

papers.title: The International Consensus Classification of Mature Lymphoid Neoplasms: a report from the Clinical Advisory Committee
grounds: ICC definition of high-grade B-cell lymphoma with MYC and BCL2 rearrangements (double-hit by FISH); companion/alternative framework to WHO-HAEM5 for the double-hit vs MYC-amplification vs double-expressor distinction

papers.title: Safety and efficacy of CAR T-cell therapy in central nervous system lymphoma: a systematic review and meta-analysis
grounds: CD19 CAR-T in CNS lymphoma (primary and secondary): pooled ORR 0.75, CR 0.52, CRS 83.5% (gr>=3 5.8%), ICANS 44.9% (gr>=3 17.4%), efficacy comparable PCNSL vs SCNSL. Strongest quantitative pooled anchor (38-study meta-analysis) for CAR-T in secondary CNS lymphoma

papers.title: CAR T-cell Therapy for Central Nervous System Lymphoma
grounds: CD19 CAR-T in primary and secondary CNS lymphoma (narrative review): CR 32-67%, 1-yr PFS 40-60%, neurotoxicity 36-68%; efficacy close to systemic DLBCL, tolerable neurotoxicity

papers.title: A case of successful CAR-T cell therapy for early isolated CNS recurrence of DLBCL with persistent CAR-T cells
grounds: Illustrative CD19 CAR-T (liso-cel) for isolated secondary CNS relapse of DLBCL; documents CAR-T expansion/persistence and second CR (single case report — anecdotal support only)

papers.title: Recent Bendamustine Treatment Before Apheresis Has a Negative Impact on Outcomes in Patients With Large B-Cell Lymphoma Receiving Chimeric Antigen Receptor T-Cell Therapy
grounds: Bendamustine before apheresis/CAR-T compromises T-cell fitness and outcomes: 439-patient European multicenter series; recent (<9 mo) exposure -> lower ORR (40% vs 72%), shorter PFS (1.3 vs 6.2 mo) and OS (4.6 vs 23.5 mo); grounds the sequencing hazard of bendamustine bridging before T-cell collection (tied to washout timing, not cumulative dose)

papers.title: Bayer provides update on Aliqopa (copanlisib): voluntary withdrawal of US NDA for relapsed follicular lymphoma after confirmatory trial (CHRONOS-4) failed to meet PFS endpoint
grounds: Copanlisib (Aliqopa) US market withdrawal, Nov 2023, following confirmatory-trial PFS failure — removes copanlisib as a viable option in the R/R lymphoma decision tree. CORRECTION: failed confirmatory trial was CHRONOS-4, not CHRONOS-3 (which was positive). No PMID/DOI — press-release/regulatory source only

nccn_sections_to_extract: NCCN B-Cell Lymphomas — DLBCL: Second-Line Therapy (candidates for transplant / intention to proceed to HDT+ASCT)
NCCN B-Cell Lymphomas — DLBCL: Second-Line and Subsequent Therapy (non-candidates for transplant / transplant-ineligible)
NCCN B-Cell Lymphomas — DLBCL: Third-Line and Subsequent Therapy (R/R, includes bispecifics, ADCs, tafasitamab-lenalidomide, loncastuximab)
NCCN B-Cell Lymphomas — Suggested Treatment Regimens for DLBCL (regimen tables: R-ICE, R-DHAP/DHAX, R-GDP, R-GemOx, pola-BR, tafa-len, glofitamab, epcoritamab, mosunetuzumab, loncastuximab)
NCCN B-Cell Lymphomas — Principles of CAR T-Cell Therapy (product selection, eligibility, bridging, contraindications)
NCCN B-Cell Lymphomas — CAR T-cell therapy eligibility / referral criteria within DLBCL 2L and 3L pathways
NCCN B-Cell Lymphomas — DLBCL in older/frail adults (R-miniCHOP, dose-attenuation, geriatric assessment)
NCCN B-Cell Lymphomas — High-Grade B-Cell Lymphoma with MYC and BCL2 rearrangements (double-hit) and double-expressor: workup and R/R management
NCCN B-Cell Lymphomas — Secondary CNS Lymphoma: CNS prophylaxis, evaluation, and treatment of CNS involvement (HD-MTX-based, MATRix/MARIETTA-type regimens)
NCCN B-Cell Lymphomas — Diagnosis / Workup / Immunophenotyping and molecular subtyping (cell-of-origin, FISH for MYC/BCL2/BCL6)
NCCN B-Cell Lymphomas — Allogeneic HCT positioning after CAR-T failure / for R/R disease
NCCN B-Cell Lymphomas — Principles of Radiation Therapy and Response Assessment (Lugano criteria) as applied to R/R DLBCL
gaps: Epcoritamab monotherapy phase 3 EPCORE DLBCL-1 (confirmatory trial): topline press-release only as of Jul 2026 — no peer-reviewed publication, no PMID/DOI. OS not yet reported. Update when a full paper/meeting presentation appears.
Copanlisib withdrawal: regulatory/press-release only — no PMID/DOI. Best citable source is the Bayer press release; also note the rubric correction (failed trial = CHRONOS-4, not CHRONOS-3).

No dedicated citation for the explicit claim that R-CHOP-backbone components and cisplatin do not reach cytotoxic CNS levels — Korfel 2015 is PCNSL-framed and does not headline this contrast. Needs a dedicated secondary-CNS-prophylaxis/pharmacology reference to ground the R-ICE-vs-R-DHAP CNS-penetration argument.

No study directly quantifies the incremental/cumulative peripheral neuropathy from prior vincristine (CHOP/R-CHOP) followed by polatuzumab vedotin (MMAE) — the vincristine->pola stacking concern is mechanistic (shared microtubule-inhibitor MoA) plus the FDA POLIVY label, not a head-to-head study.
FDA POLIVY Prescribing Information and the STARGLO FDA CRL (2025-07-18) are regulatory documents without PMIDs/DOIs; they support label/regulatory framing but are not fetchable as papers.
cns-pharmacology-korfel-2015 has a DOI but no cleanly linked PMID/PMCID (Expert Rev Hematol indexing gap) — fetchable by DOI only; verify retrieval.

CAR-T in secondary CNS lymphoma is grounded primarily by a meta-analysis (Shawabkeh 2026), a narrative review (Houillier 2024), and a single case report (Takigawa 2024) — no dedicated prospective/registry trial of CAR-T specifically in secondary CNS DLBCL; evidence base is inherently non-randomized.
